# Verify `sesam tell` upserts: creating a new user as before, but updating an
# existing one - replacing/merging groups and adding recipients - instead of
# failing. Creating a brand-new user still requires a recipient.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Create bob as a dev with one recipient.
exec sesam tell --user bob --recipient $BOB_PUBKEY --group dev
exec sesam user list
stdout 'bob.*\bdev\b'
exec sesam verify

# Re-tell an existing user with --group ops and no recipient: groups are
# replaced (dev -> ops), no user.tell entry is written for the re-tell.
exec sesam tell --user bob --group ops
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"user.change_groups"'
exec sesam user list
stdout 'bob.*\bops\b'
! stdout 'bob.*\bdev\b'
exec sesam verify

# --group-add merges into the current membership.
exec sesam tell --user bob --group-add dev
exec sesam user list
stdout 'bob.*dev,ops'
exec sesam verify

# Recipient-only update: no --group flag, so the group set is left untouched.
exec sesam tell --user bob --recipient $CAROL_PUBKEY
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"user.add_recipients"'
grep $BOB_PUBKEY sesam.yml
grep $CAROL_PUBKEY sesam.yml
exec sesam user list
stdout 'bob.*dev,ops'
exec sesam verify

# Updating an existing user with neither a group nor a recipient is a no-op.
exec sesam tell --user bob
exec sesam user list
stdout 'bob.*dev,ops'

# Creating a brand-new user still needs a recipient (groups are optional).
! exec sesam tell --user newbie --group dev
stderr 'without a recipient'

-- some_file.txt --
Some content
